ogTitle: Coopers Gully vs Cooplacurripa: Climate Safety

heading: Coopers Gully vs Cooplacurripa — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Coopers Gully, New South Wales and Cooplacurripa, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Coopers Gully has a lower overall climate risk score (36/100) compared to Cooplacurripa (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Coopers Gully or Cooplacurripa safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Coopers Gully has a flood risk score of 28/100, while Cooplacurripa scores 43/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Coopers Gully scores 60/100 for bushfire risk. Cooplacurripa scores 31/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Coopers Gully or Cooplacurripa?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Coopers Gully has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.45% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
